title: Pius Dea Crusades
content:

{{Quote|These gave as good as they got, and 'cold' and 'hot' wars alternated for a thousand years—while the Republic flagellated itself with inquisitions—until Pius Dea rule met its end in that famous encounter which hardly needs retelling here.|Gabrel Treon, ''Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji: A Cartel of Genes''|<ref name="TTSP">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/praji/20090205.html|text=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji|int=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji}}</ref>}}
The '''Pius Dea Crusades''', also known as '''Contispex's Crusades''', were a series of conflicts that lasted from [[11,965 BBY]] to [[10,966 BBY]], and served as the defining events of the [[Pius Dea Era]]. The [[Pius Dea]] were a prominent [[Religion/Legends|religious]] sect with [[Humanocentrism|humanocentric]] and militaristic tendencies that emerged in the [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]] prior to [[12,000 BBY/Legends|12,000 BBY]]. They installed one of their own—[[Contispex I/Legends|Contispex I]]—as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], gaining effective control of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] government and military. Having neutralized the unpopular [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], the sect unleashed a series of military crusades and inquisitions against rival [[Alien/Legends|alien]] sects over the following centuries. This brought about a protracted series of alternating hot and cold wars which contributed to interspecies tensions between the [[Human/Legends|human]]-dominated [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and the alien [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. In [[10,967 BBY]], the Pius Dea was split by the [[Seventh Alsakan Conflict|Renunciation]], which began the [[Seventh Alsakan Conflict]]. The war ended in [[10,966 BBY]] at the [[Battle of Uquine]], removing the Pius Dea from power and bringing an end to the crusades. The repercussions of the crusades, however, would have far reaching consequences in subsequent [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]].

==Prelude==
[[Pius Dea]] arose among [[Human/Legends|Humans]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]] before [[12,000 BBY/Legends|12,000 BBY]]. Following a [[Deity/Legends|deity]] simply known as [[the Goddess]], it began with a call for communities to remain incorruptible by policing themselves. [[Contispex I/Legends|Contispex]], a [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]] who rose to prominence in the [[Coruscant Merchants' Guild]], was a fanatical devotee of the Pius Dea faith, and held that fallen communities should be restored to purity by purging their irredeemable elements.<ref name="TEGtW" />

Contispex became legendary for his opposition to corruption and vice, and his intolerance for any who did not share his high standards. He was elected [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] in [[11,987 BBY]] on a platform of cleaning up the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], after the impeachment and [[assassin]]ation of Chancellor [[Pers'lya]] of [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

Elsewhere in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Republic colonization was beginning to approach [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]. The [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] was subjected to raids by [[Slaver/Legends|slavers]] throughout the [[Kymoodon Era]], which were sponsored by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] with the aim of enslaving the [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]]. [[Lexrul/Legends|Lexrul]], [[Drogheda/Legends|Drogheda]], [[Hathrox III|Hathrox]], [[Imram]] and [[Parcovey Minor]] were among those colonies targeted. The successful [[Assault on Parcovey Minor|defense of Parcovey Minor]] was one of the few bright spots for the colonists amid the raids. This generated a deep loathing of the Hutts among colonists on the Rim.<ref name="TEGtW" /><ref name="AC5">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-5-zenith-of-the-republic|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 5: Zenith of the Republic}}</ref>

==The Crusades==
===Early campaigns===
{{Quote|Here we stand, at the extremity of our exertion, having cast behind us the idols of our fathers, to once again carry to our foe the penalties due their sacrileges. Come perdition or hard vacuum, the Republic expects that every citizen will do his duty.|Supreme Chancellor Contispex, ''Fourth Inaugural Oration''|<ref name="TTSP" />}}
In [[11,965 BBY]], [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Contispex I/Legends|Contispex]] sanctioned the first of several "crusades" against rival [[Alien/Legends|alien]] sects. Tapping into the unpopularity of the Hutts among many citizens of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], Contispex sanctioned an [[First Pius Dea Crusade|invasion]] of Hutt Space, conquering a large swath of territory along the [[Ootmian Pabol/Legends|''Ootmian Pabol'']] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]. For nearly a [[Millennium/Legends|millennium]] up to [[10,966 BBY]], the Republic would be embroiled in crusades against different alien factions, who fought back, leaving [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in a permanent state of "hot" and "cold" wars.<ref name="TTSP" /> These crusades, embarked upon in the name of [[the Goddess]], were very violent, and marked a devastating period in galactic history. The [[Pius Dea]]'s [[Religion/Legends|religious]] rationale for the crusades was that chaos lurked beyond the Rim and that they were merely following The Goddess's edict to subdue it. The Pius Dea were also lured by the promise of wealth and secrets which led them to plunder conquered [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. The loot would then be ferried back to Coruscant in [[Republic Navy/Legends|naval galleons]]. Therefore, the sect justified and promoted military imperialism and privateering as a means of religious merit.<ref name="TEGtW" />

Contispex also overhauled the government of the Republic, giving fellow Pius Dea adherents prominent military and political posts and purging all those who refused to convert. Within a generation, important government posts were reserved for members of the Pius Dea hierarchy, and after two or three generations, many of those posts had become hereditary, with government duties becoming ritualised expressions of Pius Dea power. Contispex stepped down after a forty-year rule in [[11,947 BBY]] and was succeeded as Chancellor by [[Contispex II]], beginning a [[Contispex dynasty|dynastic succession]]. By the last centuries of the Pius Dea Era, every new Chancellor took the name Contispex upon assuming office, whether he was a member of the bloodline or not.<ref name="TEGtW" />

Thus with the entire resources of the Republic Navy and [[Republic Army|Army]] available to them,<ref name="TNEC" /> by [[11,920 BBY]] the Pius Dea Republic had launched [[Fourth Pius Dea Crusade|Four Crusades]] against Hutt Space, expanding the Republic's borders as far as [[Delacrix/Legends|Delacrix]], [[Nimban/Legends|Nimban]], [[Kwenn/Legends|Kwenn]] and [[Toydaria/Legends|Toydaria]]. To Pius Dea adherents, chaos lurked beyond the Rim, and it was the will of the Goddess that it be beaten back, with great treasures and rewards for those who advanced Her cause. Thousands of Pius Dea [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] and [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]] pushed beyond the frontier.<ref name="TEGtW" /> However, unhappy with the Pius Dea's fanaticism, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] [[Recusal|recused]] itself from the Republic in [[11,933 BBY]] after the [[Third Pius Dea Crusade]], retreating to the academy world of Ossus. For centuries, the Jedi disapproved of the Pius Dea's actions but had nothing to do with the Republic's affairs. However, this Recusal sparked deep divisions within the Order. Some knights errant Jedi were involved in vigilante wars against the Pius Dea while other Jedi aligned themselves with the sect, becoming the [[Order of the Terrible Glare/Legends|Order of the Terrible Glare]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

Once Pius Dea was secure at the top of the Republic, Contispex and his successors sought to purify the galaxy. [[Alien/Legends|Non-Human]] senators were arrested in witch-hunts, and [[Commissar/Legends|commissars]] were attached to military units to expose heretics and apostates in the ranks. The [[Proscribed]] were exiled or imprisoned, their property confiscated for pontifical auction, and their families blacklisted from political or military life for three generations, breaking the power of many ancient Republic dynasties.<ref name="TEGtW" />

Pius Dea soon turned their attention to other alien species and sects within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] which had been members of the Republic for millennia: the Seventh, or the [[Great Northern Crusade]] of [[11,884 BBY]] was fought against the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabraks]] and the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]] and saw the conquest of [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]], while the [[Crusade of the Wilds]] of [[11,791 BBY]] launched from [[Ord Cestus/Legends|Ord Cestus]] and [[Ord Marsax]] saw the conquest of a swath of territory north of the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]]. Finally, the sect even targeted Humans accused of being alien sympathizers: Republic citizens from [[Fedje]] fled to the [[Constellation/Legends|constellation]] they called [[Prefid's Belt]] to escape Pius Dea rule, and there founded the secret colony of [[Prefsbelt IV/Legends|Prefsbelt IV]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

For millennia, Alsakan and Coruscant had been rivals for dominance of the Republic. Due to the Pius Dea's origins on Coruscant, the planet became a stronghold of the sect. Religious tensions coalesced with political and economy rivalry to culminate in the [[Sixth Alsakan Conflict]] in [[11,820 BBY]]. During that conflict the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] of [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] and their territories in the Perlemian Trade Route seceded from the Pius Dea Republic. Many alien worlds and species, including the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], [[Herglic/Legends|Herglics]] and Hutts subsequently aligned themselves with Alsakan in return for protection from the Republic. In the aftermath of the Sixth Alsakan Conflict, [[Herglic Space/Legends|Herglic Space]] would be the target of the [[Eleventh Pius Dea Crusade]] in [[11,707 BBY]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

===Manifest Destiny===
[[File:Pius_Dea_Faithful.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Pius Dea [[Pius Dea|Faithful]] give thanks for the arrival of [[cathedral ship]]s.]]
{{Quote|The Goddess Wills It!|Rallying cry of the Crusaders|<ref name="TEGtW" />}}
By the [[11,591 BBY|11,500s BBY]], millions of worlds were subject to Pius Dea rule. The Republic Navy became a Pius Dea stronghold, and its [[cathedral ship]]s became essential to sustaining these expansionary campaigns; doubling as warships and religious temples. Due to a hereditary system of patronage, these massive warships were crewed by the descendants of naval crews who had converted to the sect. Other Republic warships at this time including armored [[Man-of-war/Legends|men-of-war]] optimised for [[Broadside battle|broadside combat]], and primitive [[Bi-wing snub fighter|bi-wing]] [[Snubfighter/Legends|snubfighters]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

During this period, several military outposts known as [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depots]] (O/RDs) were established to ostensibly protect [[Human/Legends|Human]] settlers from hostile alien threats. This further deepened interspecies tensions, with the anti-alien nature of the crusades forcing an exodus into Hutt Space and turning the [[The Slice/Legends|Rimward Slice]] into a militarized zone dominated by war profiteers.<ref name="TEA" /> These O/RDs operated as home bases for Republic Navy units and Pius Dea missionaries to launch military and religious expeditions into the frontier.<ref name="TEGtW" />

During the crusades, the main Pius Dea ''modus operandi'' was to deploy Republic naval warships carrying millions of troops to enemy worlds or "founts of chaos." Military veterans who emerged from [[Ground warfare/Legends|trench warfare]] during planetary invasions received substantial rewards for themselves and their families. Entire species deemed irredeemable, like the [[Zarracine]]s and [[Teirasan]]s in the [[Twelfth Pius Dea Crusade]] of [[11,660 BBY]], were subjected to genocide. Despite its Humanocentric origins, the sect's numbers swelled as thousands of Rimward worlds and species were forcibly converted or coerced into joining the Pius Dea. Conversion was seen as a means for alien individuals and worlds to gain favor with the Pius Dea Republic since it afforded greater autonomy and lessened interstellar travel restrictions for those joining the military. However, these alien converts were often contemptuously referred to as "Rimkin" by their human brethren.<ref name="TEGtW" />

===Denouement===
The religious zeal of the Pius Dea Republic antagonized many species and powers within the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Following the [[Sixth Alsakan Conflict]], Coruscant's rival Alsakan opened secret lines of communication with several alien species alienated by the Crusades including the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], [[Herglic/Legends|Herglics]], and the Hutts. By the 11,100s BBY, the Republic's military expansionism had stalled and the Pius Dea leadership began internal [[Inquisition]]s to root out apostates, heretics, and unbelievers within their ranks. These inquisitions created much hardship and deaths. [[Caamasi/Legends|Caamasi]] emissaries came to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] stronghold of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] and succeeded in convincing the Order to end its recusal in [[11,100 BBY]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

The Jedi subsequently collaborated with the [[Caamasi/Legends|Caamasi]] and Alsakani to engineer a schism within the Pius Dea adherents, spreading a secret heresy whose followers became known as the [[Renunciates]]. One such Renunciate, the Republic Navy [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Pers Pradeux]], discovered the location of [[Prefsbelt IV/Legends|Prefsbelt IV]] in the 10,970s BBY and made it into a retreat for fellow Renunciate naval officers, where they plotted the overthrow of the Pius Dea Republic.<ref name="TEGtW" />

In [[10,967 BBY]], these religious heretics announced themselves during the Renunciation; creating a [[Seventh Alsakan Conflict|vicious civil war]] which wrecked the Republic. This allowed a broad anti-Pius Dea coalition led by the Jedi to assail the Pius Dea Republic. While much of the Republic Navy defected to join the Renunciates, the Pius Dea still maintained a large fleet of thousands of [[cathedral ship]]s which they used to wage war on their enemies. In [[10,966 BBY]], the sect was militarily defeated when the [[Bureau of Ships and Services/Legends|Bureau of Ships and Services]] seeded the Pius Dea fleet with rogue navicomputer codes which caused much of the fleet to leap into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Many of these cathedral ships would remain trapped forever in the depths of unexplored space, unable to restart their engines. Those trapped aboard subsequently perished from starvation and lack of supplies.<ref name="TEGtW" />

The crusades finally ended after the [[Battle of Uquine]] which saw the annihilation of what remained of the Pius Dea fleet and the capture of Supreme Chancellor [[Contispex XIX]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> The Jedi subsequent took over the machinery of the Republic with [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Biel Ductavis]] assuming the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|chancellorship]]. Meanwhile, all members of the sect were removed from positions of power including the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] and the Pius Dea gradually died out within a few generations.<ref name="TEGtW" />

==Aftermath==
The subsequent period, the [[Ductavis Era]], became remembered as a time of rebuilding in the aftermath of the Crusades. Interspecies relations improved to the point that during the subsequent [[Rianitus Period]], a Hutt even served as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]. [[Blotus]] the Hutt served for a 275-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] term and became remembered as one of the finest Supreme Chancellors in Republic history.<ref name="TEA" />

Despite this, tensions between the [[Human/Legends|Human]]-dominated Core Worlds and the predominantly [[Alien/Legends|alien]] Rimward territories lingered for millennia. The existence of the [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depots]], explicitly created by the [[Pius Dea]] to protect human [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]], was a particular source of aggravation, especially the continued use of the prefix "''Ord''" in many [[Planet/Legends|planets']] names.<ref name="TEA" /> The tensions from the fallout of the crusades would deepen the rifts between the Core Worlds and those of the Outer Rim, which [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] would come to exploit during his reign as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] thousands of years later.<ref name="TNEC" />

The Republic Navy's assistance in overthrowing Pius Dea helped restore some of the service's honor following its millennium of theocratic control. [[Pers Pradeux]] and his fellow officers reorganized and rebuilt the navy in a series of meetings on [[Prefsbelt IV/Legends|Prefsbelt IV]] in the aftermath of the [[Battle of Uquine]], and as the Republic rebuilt, the navy became a pillar of its strength and one of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] most respected institutions.

==Timeline of the Pius Dea Crusades==
*[[11,987 BBY]]
**[[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Pers'lya]] is impeached by the [[Pius Dea]] sect and replaced by Pius Dea favorite [[Contispex I/Legends|Contispex I]].<ref name="TTSP" />

*[[11,965 BBY]]
**The [[First Pius Dea Crusade]]. Contispex I sanctions a military invasion of Hutt Space, beginning the Pius Dea Crusades.<ref name="TTSP" />

*[[11,947 BBY]]
**Contispex I steps down as Supreme Chancellor and is succeeded by his son [[Contispex II]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[11,939 BBY]]
**The [[Third Pius Dea Crusade]]. Contispex II sanctions yet another invasion of Hutt Space, instigating the Third crusade.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[11,933 BBY]]
**The [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] begins its [[Recusal]], retreating to [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] and severing all ties with the Republic.<ref name="TEGtW" /> The Jedi still loyal to the Pius Dea cult form the [[Order of the Terrible Glare/Legends|Order of the Terrible Glare]] and settle on [[Garn/Legends|Garn]].

*[[11,920 BBY]]
**The [[Fourth Pius Dea Crusade]] begins against the Hutts.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[11,884 BBY]]
**The Seventh Pius Dea Crusade, also known as the [[Great Northern Crusade]], begins against the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] and other [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the northern region of the galaxy.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*c. [[11,820 BBY]]
**The [[Sixth Alsakan Conflict]] begins as the [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakani]] ally with the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], [[Herglic/Legends|Herglics]], and the Hutts against the Pius Dea-controlled Republic.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[11,791 BBY]]
**The Tenth Pius Dea Crusade, also known as the [[Crusade of the Wilds]], begins.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[11,707 BBY]]
**The [[Eleventh Pius Dea Crusade]] against the Herglics begins.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[11,660 BBY]]
**The [[Twelfth Pius Dea Crusade]] against the [[Zarracine]]s and the [[Teirasan]]s begins.<ref name="TEGtW" />
***Republic forces led by [[Commodore/Legends|Commodore]] [[Daymont Praji]] orbitally [[Bombardment of Zarracina III|bombard]] [[Zarracina III]], turning its surface into an irradiated wasteland and forcing the native [[Zarracine]]s into a nomadic existence.<ref name="TTSP" />

*[[11,591 BBY]]
**The [[Fifteenth Pius Dea Crusade]] against the [[Baragwin/Legends|Baragwin]] begins.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[11,198 BBY]]
**The [[Twenty-Third Pius Dea Crusade]] against the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] and the [[Lannik (species)/Legends|Lanniks]] begins.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*c. [[11,100 BBY]]
**The [[Inquisition]]s begin in the Core Worlds and the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="TEGtW" />
**The Jedi Order's Recusal ends.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[11,057 BBY]]
**The [[Thirty-Fourth Pius Dea Crusade]] against the Hutts begins.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[10,967 BBY]]
**The [[Seventh Alsakan Conflict|Renunciation]]: The [[Renunciates|Renunciate]] movement reveals itself and allies with the Alsakani, [[Caamasi/Legends|Caamasi]], and the Jedi Order. This splits the Pius Dea faith from within, pitting the Faithful against those who have abandoned the faith.<ref name="TEGtW" />
**Renunciate forces clash with Faithfuls at [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]], [[Ixtlar/Legends|Ixtlar]], and [[Cyrillia/Legends|Cyrillia]], supported by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Alsakani, Duros, Herglics, and Hutts.<ref name="TEGtW" />

*[[10,966 BBY]]
**The [[Bureau of Ships and Services/Legends|Bureau of Ships and Services]] seed the Republic Navy with rogue navicomputer codes, which forces the Pius Dea's ships to jump randomly to hyperspace and become lost forever.<ref name="TEGtW" />
**At the [[Battle of Uquine]], the Renunciate forces annihilate the remaining Pius Dea ships. The Jedi capture [[Contispex XIX]] aboard the ''[[Flame of Sinthara]]'', taking him to [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]] where he stands trial and is imprisoned.<ref name="TEGtW" />
**[[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Biel Ductavis]] assumes the position of Supreme Chancellor.<ref name="TEGtW" />
